evals.prompt: i need to generate all the figures for the paper. the experiments are done and analysis is complete. where do the figures go and what will they look like?
evals.expected_output: Generates all 4 figure types, explains what each shows, and where they are saved.
expectations: Runs or suggests python -m src.generate_figures all
Explains all 4 figure types (accuracy curves, quadrant scatter, cost heatmap, rank stability)
Tells user where figures are saved (figures/ directory)
Mentions both PDF and PNG output formats
Describes what to look for in each figure
Handles dynamic model sets from the configured registry

evals.prompt: the accuracy curves figure doesn't look right - the lines are all overlapping and hard to read. can you help me fix it?
evals.expected_output: Reads the figure generation code, suggests adjustments to improve readability.
expectations: Reads src/generate_figures.py to understand current implementation
Suggests concrete adjustments (line styles, figure dimensions, alpha, markers)

evals.prompt: generate the cost heatmap. i want to see which model and intervention combinations are the most cost-effective for recovering accuracy
evals.expected_output: Generates the cost heatmap figure using registry-backed pricing data.
expectations: Runs or suggests python -m src.generate_figures cost_heatmap
Explains what the heatmap axes represent (models x interventions)
Notes that cost data comes from model_registry pricing
Describes how to interpret the cost-effectiveness quadrants
Handles whatever models are configured in the registry
